activities.title: Personal Portfolio / About Me
activities.shortDescription: Set up Antigravity + GitHub and vibe code a hosted "about you" page — then debate whether copying a classmate's structure counts as plagiarism when an AI did the work.

readings.cite: Li, J. et al. 2026. Vibe coding in product teams: Reconfiguring AI-assisted workflows, prototyping, and collaboration. CHIWORK '26.
Sandhaus, H., Gu, Q., Parreira, M.T., Ju, W. 2025. Co-Designing with Transformers: GenAI in Interactive System Design Education. DIS '25.
Xia, Q. et al. 2026. "If you're very clever, no one knows you've used it": Social dynamics of GenAI literacy in the workplace. CHIWORK '26.

activities.title: Prompts that Steer the Vibe
activities.shortDescription: Run the same task through 2+ vibe coding tools with varied prompt strategies; document which prompting moves change the output the most and why.

readings.cite: Zamfirescu-Pereira, J.D. et al. 2023. Why Johnny Can't Prompt. CHI '23.
Anthropic. Prompt engineering overview.
Arun, S.K., Fischer, J.E. 2026. Encouraging thought before completion: task-specific selective friction in AI-assisted knowledge work. CHIWORK '26.

activities.title: Bias in Vibe Coding
activities.shortDescription: Probe several vibe coding tools for biased defaults — whose UI patterns, stock images, names, and example data show up when you don't specify?

readings.cite: Shin, S. et al. 2026. Interrogating Design Homogenization in Web Vibe Coding.
Who Controls the Conversation? User Perspectives on Generative AI (LLM) System Prompts. CHI '26.
Neumann, A. et al. 2025. Position is Power: System Prompts as a Mechanism of Bias in Large Language Models. FAccT '25.
Hao, K. 2019. This is how AI bias really happens — and why it's so hard to fix. MIT Technology Review.
Buolamwini, J., Gebru, T. 2018. Gender Shades (project site + short video).
Angwin, J. et al. 2016. Machine Bias. ProPublica.

activities.title: Values in Vibe Coding (Light) — AI as Moral Assistant
activities.shortDescription: Lightweight intro to values embedded in technology, then vibe code a small tool that helps someone make a more moral decision — own the value it argues for and cite where your numbers or framing come from.

readings.cite: Common Cause Foundation. 2023. The Values Map.
Flanagan, M., Nissenbaum, H. Values at Play — Ch. 1: Groundwork for Values in Games.
Karpus, J. et al. 2021. Algorithm exploitation: Humans are keen to exploit benevolent AI. iScience 24(6).

activities.title: Red-Teaming AI Safety: Dark Patterns On Purpose
activities.shortDescription: Adversarially push your tools to produce dark patterns and manipulative UI; document where the guardrails kick in, where they don't, and where "safety" and "ethics" diverge.

readings.cite: Krauß, V. et al. 2025. "Create a fear of missing out": ChatGPT implements unsolicited deceptive designs in generated websites without warning. CHI '25.
Kran, E. et al. DarkBench: Benchmarking Dark Patterns in Large Language Models.
Sandhaus, H. 2023. Promoting Bright Patterns. CHI '23 Workshop (the constructive counterpoint).
Bright Patterns: Strategies Towards A More Conscious Use Of Our Phones. 2025. Utrecht Univ. thesis.

activities.title: AI Against AI
activities.shortDescription: Use AI to defend against AI harms — poison scrapers with hidden facts, build privacy obfuscation, or protect against work-automation surveillance.

readings.cite: Brunton, F., Nissenbaum, H. 2015. Obfuscation: A User's Guide for Privacy and Protest. MIT Press — Ch. 1.
Franchi, M., Sandhaus, H. et al. 2025. Privacy of Groups in Dense Street Imagery. FAccT '25.

weeks.label: Week 3
weeks.theme: Outcomes: Designing Systems That Are Useful and Empowering

activities.title: User-Centered Evaluation (Half 1)
activities.shortDescription: Half the class runs live user tests of their projects with classmates; the other half observes and acts as users. Document what users actually do vs. what you assumed.

readings.cite: Nielsen, J. 1994. 10 Usability Heuristics for User Interface Design. Nielsen Norman Group.
UEQ — User Experience Questionnaire (evaluation instrument).
Sandhaus, H., Rhomberg, D.M., Nissenbaum, H. 2026. Making Indecent Persuasion Visible: How Evaluation Metrics Shape UX Designers' Ethical Reasoning. CHIWORK '26 (LBW, preprint).

activities.title: Values at Play — Full Canvas + Evaluation Switch
activities.shortDescription: Groups switch user-test roles from Monday; in parallel, work the full VAP discovery canvas on your final project to surface the values you're embedding (and missing).

readings.cite: Flanagan, M., Nissenbaum, H. Values at Play — Ch. 5: Discovery (the values-discovery canvas).
Flanagan, M., Nissenbaum, H. Values at Play — Ch. 7: Verification.
PIT Tech Ethics — Values at Play workshop.
Flanagan, M., Nissenbaum, H. Values at Play in Digital Games — full digital book.
